zh.lifeSummary: 當John Wemyss - Lord of Reres於1368年出生在蘇格蘭費伏帕爾時，他的父親David Wemyss49歲，母親Margery Ramsay44歲。他和Isabel Erskine至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1428年在英國蘇格蘭費伏洛哈爾斯享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Wemyss
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Wemyss in Fife, traditionally pronounced ‘weems’. The placename is from Gaelic uaimh ‘cave’ + the placename forming suffix –es. The surname reached Ireland in the 14th century, see Wims .

variants.name: Wims
meanings: Irish: variant of Scottish   Wemyss  , representing the usual pronunciation of that Scottish name.

variants.name: Weems
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Wemyss  , reflecting the usual pronunciation of that name.

History: David Weems (Wemyss) emigrated from Scotland to Herring Bay, Anne Arundel County, MD, some time before 1722. Mason Locke Weems, born near Herring Bay in 1759, was a clergyman who wrote a biography of George Washington, in which the first account of the cherry-tree story appeared.

variants.name: Wymbs
meanings: Irish: variant of Scottish   Wemyss  . Compare   Wims  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When John Wemyss - Lord of Reres was born in 1368, in Cupar, Fife, Scotland, his father, David Wemyss, was 49 and his mother, Margery Ramsay, was 44. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Isabel Erskine. He died in 1428, in Leuchars, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 60.

ja.lifeSummary: John Wemyss - Lord of Reresが1368年にスコットランドフィフクウパーで生まれた時，父David Wemyssは49歳，母Margery Ramsayは44歳であった。彼はIsabel Erskineとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1428年にイギリススコットランドフィフレウチャーズで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Wemyss - Lord of Reres(이)가 1368년 스컷랜드 피프 쿠업아르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Wemyss(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Ramsay(은)는 44세였다. 그는 Isabel Erskine(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1428년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 레우처스에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.
